Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Mar-21-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Matthew Talbot, Esq., for Steven Kmucha, Administrator.
No other appearances.

At the outset the court notes the absence of attorney for Respondents, John Chu. Mr. Talbot reports of his most recent communication with Mr. Chu. At the court's query, counsel reports as to the following issues: there is no DE-120 form is on file and of a possible encumbrance on the Estate property. After additional discussion, the court takes the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

The court and counsel then discuss whether the hearings on the administrator's 850 petition and status on the close of this estate currently set for Jul-08-2025 should be continued as the estate may not receive settlement money until after that hearing as the agreement requires payment within 120 days after the Court approves this petition. A future date is discussed. After consideration, the previously mentioned matters are continued from Jul-08-2025 to Aug-06-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Talbot advises that he will notify Mr. Chu regarding the continued matters.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Continued To Nov-18-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew B. Talbot, via Zoom, for and with Steven Kmucha, Petitioner; Peter Bresler, via Zoom, for Qubao Yu, Petitioner.

The court advises that it appears that Qubao Yu has priority in this matter but that there are allegations that Qubao Yu has made misrepresentations of material facts to the court which would need to be ignored to appoint his client as Administrator. Mr. Bresler offers advocacy for his client's petition and requests an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Talbot offers advocacy for his client's petition and reports on the family dynamics and that his client should be appointed as Administrator. There is discussion regarding the adoption issue in this matter with Mr. Talbot mentioning equitable adoption as a material fact. The court disagrees with Mr. Talbot's position and advises that it will be setting an evidentiary hearing on the sole issue of adoption.

The matters are continued to Nov-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An evidentiary hearing on the issue stated above is set for the same date. Counsel is advised that they will each be provided 1.5 hours to provided evidence with Petitioner beginning and ending the hearing as they have the burden of proof. The court summarizes what will be expected at the hearing. Mr. Bresler advises that due to physical limitations, he would need to appear via Zoom or have another attorney specially appear for him. The court advises the parties that they should meet and confer regarding the remote appearance issue and file a stipulation for remote appearance if needed.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
